$schema_note: Canonical governed audio asset pack. Companion to canonical-image-pack-manifest.json -- that manifest's own $schema_note documents excluding these exact 90 storyboard narration MP3 files ('audio/*, video/*, and non-image artifacts... 90 mp3 voice files') by design, since it is validated (tests/test_release_fix_a3_canonical_image_pack.py::test_manifest_contains_only_image_mime_types, tests/test_release_fix_a2_asset_closure.py) to contain image/* MIME types only. This manifest is the single source of truth for which audio files under assets/storyboards/ a static release generation stages -- see deploy/live-static-asset-inventory.json's required_subtrees for how it is wired in, and docs/deployment/canonical_static_narration_audio_contract.md for the full rationale.
provenance_summary: All 90 files restored from historical commit 2efdb6e3c in PR #107 (fix(assets): restore referenced storyboard narration, d79a050ea) and verified byte-identical to the committed source at HEAD via git cat-file blob SHA-256 comparison.

files.source_evidence: Committed in fix(assets): restore referenced storyboard narration (d79a050ea, PR #107), byte-verified against historical commit 2efdb6e3c (or its verified byte-identical archived copy for the single object affected by a transient git pack read issue -- see 10c730985 in PR #107). Governed here so package-static-release.ps1 stages it in every static release generation (previously excluded, see canonical-image-pack-manifest.json's $schema_note: 'Excludes audio/*... 90 mp3 voice files').
